Shouldn't matter - `rm -fa` does stop all containers that are running
If it does change things, we have a serious bug in `pod rm`
Right this PR would cover up for a broken podman rm -a -f

Not necessarily.  We don't have root cause. It could be environmental.  I cannot reproduce this locally running the same scripts, etc.  I have questions about the pod rm code with just a quick glance.
